presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log highlights gaps in vendor control evidence requiring remediation, while the Privileged Account Approval Email establishes a controlled, auditable pilot for privileged access with strict scoping, logging, and mandatory quarterly revalidation. The former addresses incomplete evidence blocking control closure, whereas the latter enforces proactive governance for approved access.

- Incomplete evidence blocks control closure until provenance is verified.  
- Status indicators must be confirmed and artifacts archived in the approved repository.  
- Standing privileged access is approved for two named administrators during a pilot.  
- Access is tightly scoped to operational tasks with strict logging requirements.  
- Mandatory quarterly recertification and vendor confirmation of personnel are required.  
- Governance mandates revalidation if personnel change.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log highlights gaps in vendor control evidence requiring remediation, while the Privileged Account Approval Email establishes a controlled, auditable pilot for privileged access with strict scoping, logging, and mandatory quarterly revalidation. The former addresses incomplete evidence blocking control closure, whereas the latter enforces proactive governance for approved access.

Mandatory quarterly recertification and vendor confirmation of personnel are required.
